feed.title: SAP Community - SAP HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to map AD group to roles in HANA Cockpit
entry.content:
How the AD group are mapped with the standard roles available in the HANA Cockpit also need to know how to get the details of user to role. 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Allocation Number on Bank Statement SAP HANA
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I'm in the Bank Statement Management application F1564, where I ran the transaction and entered the assignment number as 0. However, when I check FB03, it saves a different assignment number. I made the correction manually.

Bank Statement

FB03

Is that number generated automatically? How can I correct it so it shows the bank statement assignment number?

SAP S/4HANA Finance

entry.title: HANA ODBC Test Connection Error
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm currently trying to establish an ODBC connection to a SAP HANA Cloud database, I need this to connect the data to a Microsoft PowerBI report. However, no matter what I try, the following message appears everytime I try to test the connection:

"Communication Link Failure:-10709 connection failed (RTE:[89006] system call 'connect' failed, rc=10061, No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it)."

Please consider the following:

  • My computer can successfully ping the SAP Hana server I'm trying to connect to, via cmd.
  • The credentials needed to test the ODBC connection are disregarded, it makes no difference if I use the correct credentials or purposefully wrong ones, the connection will show the same error.

#UPDATE 1:

The original issue was resolved, using telnet instead of ping I managed to identify that the original server I was trying to connect to, wasn't answering correctly. Now using a secondary server which succesfully responds to the telnet command. However, a new issue has shown up, the following error message appears whenever I try to test the connection:

Any help or advice is greatly appreciated.

Thanks,

Daniel.

entry.title: In AMDP, is it possible to use a SEQUENCE object? (nextval)
entry.content:
I'd like to do a SELECT query in an AMDP method with one column being a number starting at 1, incremented by 1, without gaps.

As ROW_NUMBER is not guaranteed to avoid gaps, I guess I should use a SEQUENCE HANA object that I reset to 1 before running the query. For using a sequence, I need to use HANA SQL, so either I use native SQL, or I use a CDS table function and AMDP.

With native SQL, it works:

REPORT.
DATA dummy_column TYPE c LENGTH 1.
DATA autoincrement TYPE i.
DATA error TYPE REF TO cx_root.

TRY.
    EXEC SQL.
      drop sequence zmysequence;
    ENDEXEC.
  CATCH cx_sy_native_sql_error INTO error ##NO_HANDLER ##NEEDED.
ENDTRY.

TRY.
    EXEC SQL.
      create sequence zmysequence start with 1 increment by 1;
    ENDEXEC.
  CATCH cx_sy_native_sql_error INTO error ##NO_HANDLER ##NEEDED.
ENDTRY.

TRY.
    EXEC SQL PERFORMING test.
      select dummy_column, 
             zmysequence.nextval autoincrement
          from ( select 'A' dummy_column from dummy
                 union select 'B' dummy_column from dummy )
          into :dummy_column, :autoincrement
    ENDEXEC.
  CATCH cx_sy_native_sql_error INTO error ##NO_HANDLER ##NEEDED.
ENDTRY.

ASSERT 1 = 1. " dummy statement for setting a breakpoint (if debug needed)

FORM test.
  WRITE : / dummy_column, autoincrement.
ENDFORM.

Now, let's try with AMDP. It doesn't allow sequences:

Error without USING:

"ZMYSEQUENCE" is unknown. ABAP objects and DDIC objects must be declared in the USING clause of the method. Local names must start with ":" here.

Error with USING:

"ZMYSEQUENCE" is invalid. Only transparent database tables, views, or AMDP methods can be specified after USING.

Is there a way to define an autoincrement with AMDP?

entry.title: ROW_NUMBER does gaps via AMDP but no gaps via ABAP SQL, bug in my code?
entry.content:
In ABAP 7.58, I have written an SQL query with ROW_NUMBER( ) OVER( ), in two versions, AMDP and pure ABAP SQL.

If Via AMDP HANA SQL, I obtain gaps in the numbers, but no gaps via ABAP SQL, why is there a difference?

1. Via AMDP/CDS Table Function, called from ABAP via SELECT * FROM zcds WHERE bukrs = '1000' :

return select 
  bkpf.bukrs,
  bkpf.waers,
  ...
  sum( ... ) as wrbtr,
  ...
  cast(' ' as belnr_d) as extbelnr,
  ...
  row_number() over ( ) as idnum,
from acdoca
  inner join ...
where ... // conditions against constants
group by ... // all fields before the sum fields
;

2. Via ABAP SQL:

INSERT zzz FROM ( select 
  ROW_NUMBER( ) OVER( ) AS ROW_NUMBER,
  bkpf~bukrs,
  bkpf~waers,
  ...
  sum( ... ) as wrbtr,
  ...
  cast(' ' as char( 10 ) ) as extbelnr,
  ...
from acdoca
  inner join ...
where bukrs = '1000'
  and ... // conditions against constants
group by ... // all fields before the sum fields
).

Thanks

Sandra

My system: ABAP 7.58

entry.title: Purchase order with history release strategy is restarted
entry.content:
Hi Experts

The release strategy of the released purchase order with partial goods receipt is restarted when the net amount of the purchase order is reduced.

The reduction of the amount happens because some of the items were cancelled or the quantity purchased in the item with partial attention was reduced and the new amount is in the range of another release strategy.

In the configuration of the release strategy for the release indicator is assigned the value 4 in the modification option

The business requirement is that the release strategy is only restarted when the purchase order has no goods receipt or service entry sheet. Even if the purchase order is not yet fulfilled, the release strategy should not be restarted if the new reduced amount is in the range of another strategy.

I look forward to your support if the above can be achieved with the standard configuration or if customization is required.

Best regards Percy

entry.title: Converting transfer requests with the tx ME59N
entry.content:
Hi Experts

In S/4HANA, as standard, it is possible to convert stock transport requisitions to stock transport orders using transaction ME59N, just as is done with purchase requisitions for goods.

If possible you can help me by telling me in a general way the main configurations to be done and if you could share me a user manual it would be great.

Best regards Percy

entry.title: Native HANA / XSA CDS - annotation for 'measure'
entry.content:
Hi experts,

   working on a HANA 1.0 (1.120) on premise Database and looking into what we can achieve directly on the DB for applications not running an ABAP layer.

Is it possible to define a column for a Native HANA CDS as a Measure, allowing the OData service to effectively request a dynamic "group by" based on the columns (Dimensions) requested in the query?

Example:

TestCDS.hdbdd file that does not define the Measure;

namespace IHA.models.MII.TAB_VIEWS.TD.services;
@SCHEMA: 'SAPD33'
context TestCDS {
view TestCDS as select from RESB
{
RSNUM,
RSPOS,
AUFNR,
MATNR,
BDMNG
};
};

 

Ideally I want to define BDMNG as a measure, however replacing this with @DefaultAggregation: #SUM BDMNG just results in an error when saving via the HANA Web IDE

 

The OData service is defined as;

service namespace "pp.mii.resb" {
"IHA.models.MII.TAB_VIEWS.TD.services::TestCDS.TestCDS"
as "reservationdata" keys generate local "ID"
;
}
annotations {
enable OData4SAP;
}

 

I read that it may also be possible to define the expression within the OData service (https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/advanced/odata#expression-annotations) but I cannot see how to achieve this within my OData defintion.

 

Am I attempting something only possible in an ABAP CDS/OData service or is this possible directly at DB level?

entry.title: RELEASE STRATEGY PURCHASE REQUISITION
entry.content:
Currently, the criteria for determining the release of the requisition are as follows: document type, plant, requisitioner's name and amount. The release is applied per item.

The business requires that for a given requisition type, no release strategy is applied when the item has a contract assigned to it.

I have reviewed the CEBAN structure and used the VRTYP field in a new feature, which I added to the corresponding class; however, the expected result is not obtained.

I thank you in advance for your support and look forward to your comments on how to implement this business need.

Best regards Percy

entry.title: Is it possible to obtain native SQL query that is executed over the graphical Calculation View?
entry.content:
Hello! I'm trying to find out, if it is possible to get SQL query, that will be executed on the DB, when user gets data from the Calculation View?

I know how to obtain XML definition of the Calculation View, but I believe eventually data from the DB is retrieved by running SQL query to the database. Is there a way how to get this SQL query for the Calculation view? Ideally programmatically.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Documentation of the XML structure of the Calculation view
entry.content:
Hello, is there a reasonably documented XML definition of the Calculation Views for SAP HANA? Like what are possible nodes, attributes, inner references, etc.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST does not update ACDOCA when PO is added
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m working with a custom Z development that performs mass posting of accounting documents from Excel files using the BAPI BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST. Recently, an enhancement was added to include a Purchase Order (PO) in the vendor line item through a BAdI. The issue is that when posting documents that contain the PO number in that line, the document is created but not saved in table ACDOCA, nor is it visible in the Universal Journal. It is only recorded in the classic tables (such as BKPF/BSEG), which prevents it from being cleared later, as the system doesn’t recognize it as a valid document in the Universal Ledger. Interestingly, documents that do not include a PO are posted correctly and appear in ACDOCA without any issues. Has anyone experienced a similar case? Do you know of any SAP Note or configuration that should be checked to ensure that the BAPI posts correctly when a PO is included? The enhancement used to add the PO and item is Extension 2. Any help or experience is greatly appreciated. Thank you in advance!

entry.title: How to correct non-reconcilable documents to insert them properly in ACDOCA and FAGLFLEXA?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am working on a Z development that performs mass upload of accounting documents using the BAPI BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST. Recently, we discovered that documents containing a purchase order do not get saved in the necessary tables (such as ACDOCA and FAGLFLEXA), which prevents them from being reconciled later. Documents without a purchase order are posted correctly and appear in both ACDOCA and FAGLFLEXA, but those containing the PO are not inserted into these tables, causing a problem for reconciliation. Has anyone experienced this kind of situation? Are there any methods or SAP Notes to insert these documents into ACDOCA and FAGLFLEXA tables to make them reconcilable? Any help or suggestions would be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Golden Arrow not showing on Alert
entry.content:
Hi can you please help on my query - the golden arrow is not showing on Alert - I need the golden arrow on the doc number?

Thank you.

SELECT T0."DocNum", T0."CardCode", T0."CardName", T0."DocTotal", T0."DocDate" FROM OINV T0 WHERE
T0."DocDate" = CURRENT_DATE
and
T0."DocTotal" <= 0

entry.title: STO PO creation through BAPI
entry.content:
Hi All,

           I'm creating STO PO through BAPI_PO_CREATE1, while executing this I'm facing ME 083 Enter supplier error. I have maintained correct document type, suppling plant and other details whatever we maintain during manual creation of STO PO. Can you suggest any solution?

entry.title: Negative Balances in AR Aging Report Due to FBRA on Partial Clearings (SAP S/4HANA)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team, 

We’re facing an ongoing issue in our custom AR Aging report. After running FBRA (on 12.02.2025) to reverse incorrect partial clearings from 2022 for Customer 100009, the Aging report now shows negative balances in retro reports (2022 and 2023).

These DZ (clearing reversal) documents are technically correct per FI postings, but the CFO expects no negatives in the Aging Report. If we exclude these DZ docs, the report doesn’t match FS or FBL3N.

Goal: Build aging logic that matches Financial Statements and FBL3N, both retrospectively and prospectively, but avoids showing negative AR balances due to FBRA.

Any advice on handling FBRA and DZ documents in custom aging reports? Can we simulate prior status or apply dynamic logic for cleared history?

entry.title: Prerequisites for migrating BW 7.5 SPS10 from SQL Server to HANA.
entry.content:
Hello All,

As part of the heterogeneous migration process, we are required to export BW system.

Please provide the prerequisite checks that need to be carried out prior to the BW system migration to HANA.

Source details

SAP BW 7.5 SPS10
SQL DB: Microsoft SQL Server 2017
OS: Windows Server 2019

Note: in the Target
The operating system is changing from Windows to Linux.
BW database will be changed in the target system (from sql server to HANA DB).

Regards
Appuru

entry.title: Reforma tributária, RVABRA nota 3556404, off set?
entry.content:
Os bcset da nota 3556404 entregaram as condições CBS3, IB3S e IB3M amarradas as chaves de conta MWC, MWD, MWE.

Contudo, não foi criada sua respectiva off-set, para mapping na VKOA, gerando a perninha do recolher e do a pagar, sendo uma pela OB40 e outra pela VKOA.

Acreditam que a alguma razão fiscal, ou foi deixado para ser feito Z a gosto do cliente? 

Se estiver sendo ignorante me perdoem, pois me chamou a atenção ao fazer o paralelo entre MW2 e a BRI, por exemplo.

Abraços.

Eduardo

entry.title: Checklist Before HANA Upgrade (Brownfield)
entry.content:
Hi SAP experts,

I have a customer who will be transitioning from SAP ECC to SAP HANA. A brownfield migration is planned. In this context, what is the easiest way to identify the developments and enhancements made in the ECC system? What should I do to adjust these developments after the system is upgraded to HANA?

I've gathered some information through research, but I'm looking for comprehensive knowledge. I'm open to your valuable suggestions.

 

Kind regards,
Nedim YILMAZ

entry.title: Insert ROW-TYPE variable into table without explicitly typing all column names
entry.content:
Hello all,
As far as I understand triggers hold the record about to be inserted/updated as a row variable which I am able to use by referencing its parts. Example:

CREATE TRIGGER TEST_TRIGGER_VAR_UPDATE
AFTER UPDATE ON TARGET
REFERENCING NEW ROW mynewrow, OLD ROW myoldrow
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
INSERT INTO SAMPLE_new VALUES(:mynewrow.a, :mynewrow.b);
END;

This is unacceptable for me as I want to create triggers on 50+ tables and I cannot explicitly state all of the collections elements for all these tables.
I need sth like INSERT INTO SAMPLE_new VALUES(:mynewrow.*);
or
INSERT INTO SAMPLE_new SELECT * FROM (:mynewrow);

Thanks
